title: Yemen reports oil tanker hijacked off Shabwa coast heading toward Somali waters
summary: Yemen reported an oil tanker hijacked off the Shabwa coast now heading toward Somali waters, raising maritime security concerns in the Gulf of Aden amid broader regional tensions from the Iran-US conflict.
contents:
## Oil Tanker Hijacked Off Yemen's Shabwa Coast

### Incident Report
Yemeni authorities reported the hijacking of an oil tanker off the coast of Shabwa province on May 2. The vessel changed course toward Somali waters after the seizure. Details on the crew complement and vessel identity remain limited as regional naval forces monitor the situation.

### Regional Maritime Context
The hijacking occurs against a backdrop of heightened maritime insecurity across the broader Middle East and East African waters. The ongoing US-Iran conflict and Houthi activities have dramatically increased threat levels for commercial shipping throughout the Red Sea, Gulf of Aden, and Arabian Sea corridors.

### Impact on Shipping
The incident adds to an already strained maritime security environment where commercial vessels face multiple overlapping threats. Insurance premiums for regional transit continue climbing, and several major shipping lines have rerouted vessels around the Cape of Good Hope to avoid the area.

### Gulf of Guinea Implications
While this incident occurred outside the Gulf of Guinea MDAT area of responsibility, it demonstrates the expanding geographic scope of maritime piracy and hijacking threats affecting global shipping lanes. West African maritime security stakeholders monitor these developments for potential pattern migration.

**Source:** Reuters maritime alerts
